devices.name: Bitaxe Gamma
setupPool: Connect device to power via USB-C (60W+)
Connect to WiFi via web interface at bitaxe.local
Select mining pool (ViaBTC or Slush Pool recommended)
Enter your Bitcoin wallet address and start mining
setupSolo: Flash solo mining firmware via web interface
Configure for Solo CK Pool (solo.ckpool.org:3333)
Either run Bitcoin Core locally or use pool's full node
Wait for block discovery (statistically ~847 years average)
devices.description: The perfect starter miner. Silent enough for your desk, educational, and capable of both pool and solo mining. Best value for beginners.

devices.name: NerdQaxe++ 4.8T
setupPool: Connect 120W power supply (included)
Connect ethernet cable or configure WiFi
Access web interface at nerdqaxe.local
Configure pool settings and wallet address
setupSolo: Enable solo mode in firmware settings
Point to Solo CK Pool or local Bitcoin Core
Ensure stable internet connection
Expected time to block: ~106 years on average
devices.description: The efficiency king of home miners. 8x the hashrate of Bitaxe while using only 33% more power. Best GH/s per watt in its class.

devices.name: Avalon Nano 3
setupPool: Plug into wall outlet with included PSU
Download AvalonMiner app or access web interface
Scan for device on local network
Enter pool URL and wallet address - that's it!
devices.description: The easiest home miner to set up. From Canaan, makers of industrial Avalon miners. Perfect for pool-only operators who want simplicity.

devices.name: Antminer S9 (Refurbished)
setupPool: Requires 240V 15A circuit or 4x 120V 15A circuits
Connect to network via ethernet (WiFi not recommended)
Find device IP on router or use IP scanner
Configure via web interface with pool and wallet
setupSolo: Same electrical requirements as pool mode
Configure for Solo CK Pool or local node
Ensure adequate cooling and ventilation
Expected time to block: ~36 years on average
devices.description: The classic Bitcoin miner. Cheap to acquire but power-hungry and LOUD. Only suitable for garages, sheds, or industrial spaces. Real ASIC experience.

devices.name: Bitaxe Alpha
setupPool: Connect via USB-C to 30W+ power source
Access web config at bitaxe.local
Enter WiFi credentials
Configure pool and wallet in settings
setupSolo: Enable solo firmware via web interface
Point to Solo CK Pool
Use pool's Bitcoin node or run your own
Expected time to block: ~1694 years on average
devices.description: The budget entry point. Half the hashrate of Gamma but even cheaper. Perfect for learning or as a Bitcoin-themed desk accessory.

devices.name: NerdQaxe 2.4T
setupPool: Connect 60W power supply
Configure via web interface
Set up WiFi or ethernet connection
Enter pool details and wallet address
setupSolo: Enable solo mining in settings
Configure pool endpoint for solo mode
Ensure Bitcoin node connectivity
Expected time to block: ~212 years on average
devices.description: The middle child. Good efficiency at a mid-range price. Perfect if the 4.8T is too expensive but you want more power than Bitaxe.
